title: A safety housing based implant/ medicament injecting system
abstract: A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system. System includes a needle assembly prefilled implant/medicament for injection and an injecting needle/cannula, a housing for accommodating the needle assembly under usual bias inside said housing, a plunger means having a plunger rod configured for stage wise forward motion including an initial injecting plunger forward motion with the needle assembly within the housing to first engage the needle assembly with the housing and subsequent continuing injecting plunger forward motion independent of the needle assembly for injecting of the implant/medicament, and post injecting return of the needle assembly secured inside said housing blocking any subsequent use thereof.
claims:
Claims WE CLAIM : 1. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system comprising : a needle assembly including implant/medicament for injection and an injecti ng needle/cannula ; a housing for the said needle assembly; said needle assembly with said injecting needle/cannula in said housing maintained under usualbias in said housing ; a plu nger means having a plunger rod concentrically disposed with respect to said housing and said needle assembly to effect under its forward motion a control led stepwise initial exposure of said injecti ng needle/cannula from said housing at a distal front end and followed by injecting out of the implant/medicament throug h said exposed needle/cannula; said plunger means, said housing and said needle assembly cooperatively connected such that an i nitial injecting plunger forward motion cooperates only with said needle assembly to expose the needle/cannula out of said distal front end of said housi ng and then a first engagement means engag ing the needle assembly in said position with respect to the housing to keep the needle/cannula exposed for injection and when said plunger body connection to said needle assembly is cooperatively released such that fu rther subsequent continuing injecting plunger forward motion of the plunger means facilitates the plunger rod to enter the needle assembly for said, injecting out the implant/medicament throug h the exposed need le/cannula ; a cooperative fi rst disengagement means for disengaging said housing connection to said need le assembly post injecting of the med icament/implant enabling retraction of the used needle/cannula under its said usual bias into the housing and locked therei n to avoid any future use/accidental exposure of the used needle /cannula . 2. A safety housing based i mplant/medicament injecting system as claimed i n claim 1 comprising a releasable seal means blocking any free motion of the plunger with respect to said housing until actual use. 3 , A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 1 or 2, comprises said housing having a small opening at its distal front end ; said needle assembly fully enclosed within said housing and resiliently supported with said distal front end of the housing by using a spring bias having the cannula positioned in its front end configured to eject through the opening for piercing skin, body tissue to deliver the implant/medicament; said plunger means with said plunger rod configured for the initial i njecti ng plunger forward motion to eject the needle/cannula through the opening and the subsequent continuing i njecting plunger forward motion to facilitate the plunger rod to enter in the needle assem bly to thereby inject the implant/medicament through the ejected needle/cannula ; wherein the plunger means for said initial injecting plunger forward motion involves coupling between the plunger means and the needle assembly to ensu re transferring of pushing force applied on the plunger means to the needle assembly and cooperative forward movement of the plunger means and the needle assembly inside the housing towards its front by compressing against usual biased disposition of said spring ; wherein the plunger means for said subsequent continui ng injecting plunger forward motion involves decoupling the plunger means from the back end of the needle assembly when the front end of the needle assembly is engaged with the housing involving said first engagement means at its distal front end which ena ble independent forward movement of the plunger means with respect to the needle assembly inside the housing upon continui ng pushing force applied on the plunger means and thus forward movement of the plunger rod in the needle assembly for pushing the implant/medicament through the cannula into the tissue; the first disengagement means is configured to disengage the said first engagement means between the front end of the needle assembly and the housi ng u pon complete 

 delivering of the i mplant/medicament and thereby enable the automatic retraction of the needle assembly with the cannula in the housing. 4. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 1 to 3, wherein the plunger means for the initial injecting plunger forward motion includes said releasable seal means to couple the need le assembly with the plunger means ensuring the cooperative movement of the needle assembly, the plunger and the plunger rod ; and said first engagement means to engage the front of needle assembly with the housing at its distal front end when the cannula is completely ejected through the opening and thereby arrest any forward or backward motion of the needle assembly in such extended state of the cannula for injection . 5. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 1 to 4, wherei n the plunger means for the subseq uent continuing injecting plu nger forward motion includes a seal releasing means at back end of the housi ng to disengage the coupling between needle assembly and the plunger means when the needle assembly is engaged with the housing by the first engagement means to enable independent forwa rd movement of the plu nger mea ns with respect to the need le assembly inside the housing . 6. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 1 to 5, comprises a second engagement means at the back end of the housing to permanently arrest the retracted needle assembly in the housing restricting the further use of the needle assembly and/or accidental exposu re of the used ca nnula . 7. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 1 to 6, comprises a removable protective cap on the cannula. 8. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 1 to 7, wherein the inner surface of the housing and outer surface of the needle assembly includes cooperative guiding means to arrest any unwanted rotation of the needle assembly with respect to the housing. 9. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 1 to 8, wherein the needle assembly includes forward snap which sits within the housing and configured to freely to move in the forward direction inside the housing to prevent any accidental reverse movement of the needle assembly. 10. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 1 to 9, which is push type safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system comprising said housing preferably a cylindrical barrel with a housing cap in its front end having small opening defined in the housing cap; said needle assembly fully accommodated within the housing and supported with an expanded spring positioned between housing cap and front end of the needle assembly, said needle assembly includes a needle hub comprising the cannula at its front end; a label holder at its back end; an implant container positioned in an axially symmetric manner between the label holder and the needle hub; said plunger means comprising the plunger and the plunger rod insert molded within plunger body configured for 

 the initial i njecting plunger forward motion to eject the cannula through the opening having said releasable seal means to operatively couple the plunger and the plunger rod with the label holder of the needle assembly; and said first engagement means to engage the needle hu b with the housing ; and the su bsequent continuing injecting plunger forward motion to i nject the implant/medicament through the ejected needle/cannula having the seal releasing means to disengage the coupling between the label holder and the plunger; said first disengagement means for enabling disengagement of the engaged disposition between the needle hub and the housing ; said second engaging means to permanently arrest the needle assembly in the housing post use. 1 1. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in claim 10, wherein the needle assembly comprises a plunger rod guide ru nning through the implant container in the axially symmetric manner between the label holder and the needle hub enabling the plu nger rod to move through said plunger rod guide and push the implant/medicament or injectable substances which are prefilled i n the implant container towards the needle hub. 12. A safety housing based implant/medica ment injecting system as clai med in anyone of claim 10 or 11 , wherein the ca nnula includes a fluid communicable connection with the plunger rod guide to receive the implant. 13. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 10 to 12, the need le hub is coupled with inner surface of the 

 housing by involving tongue and groove joint to arrest any rotational movement of the needle assembly with respect to the housing and allow only spring biased forward and backward motion of the needle assembly within the housing . 14. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 10 to 13, wherein the releasable seal means includes a safety clip at back end of the housing comprising a first end coupled with both the housing and the label holder and a second end externally coupled with the plunger by using a positive lock. 15. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 10 to 14, wherein the positive lock to externally couple the second end of the safety clip with the plunger includes at least one opening or slot in the plunger externally at its front end ; and at least one cooperative protrusion on the second end of safety clip configured to be detachably engaged with said opening or slot in the plunger. 16. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 10 to 15, wherein the first end of the safety clip is coupled with the housing by involving tongue and groove joint facilitating sliding of the safety clip through the housing ; and said first end of the safety clip is coupled with the label holder by involving butt joint ensuring that pushing force applied on the plunger gets transferred to the needle assembly via the safety clip and enabling cooperative forward movement of the plunger and the needle assembly inside the housing keeping the housing in static position; said cooperative movement of the plunger and the needle assembly restrict plunger rod movement through the plunger rod guide and thus prevent movement of the 

 implant independently with respect to the needle assembly during driving of the needle assembly. 17. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 10 to 16, wherein the first engagement means comprises a - snap lock on the needle hub configured to engage with cooperative locking portion in the housing front end/cap when the cannula is completely ejected out along with the protective cap from the housing with continued pushing force applied on the plunger causing the needle assembly to move towards the front end of the housing. 18. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 10 to 17, wherein the needle assembly engaged with housing as the snap lock of the needle hub gets locked with cooperative locking portion in the housing top withan audible click compressing the spring bias to arrest the forward and reverse movement of the needle assembly with respect to the housing and facilitate piercing of the cannula after removal of the protective cap into the body/skin/tissue by holding the housing. 19. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 10 to 18, wherein the label holder includes a cooperative snap within the housing to allow free movement the forward direction inside the housing and restrict the accidental reverse motion of the needle assembly. 20. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 10 to 19, wherein the seal releasing means at back end of the housing includes a mating protrusion on the housing which widens the second end of the safety clip when it interacts into said mating protrusion to detach the positive lock; and a releasing gap on the grove and tongue joint on which the first end of the safety clip slides; 

 said seal releasing means faci litates removal of the safety cl ip and enabling the plunger moveably independent from the need le assembly so that further application of pushi ng force on the plunger drive in forward direction i n the housing independent of the needle assembly causing the plunger rod to move in forward direction through the rod guide and push the implant through the cannula i nto the tissue. 21. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of clai ms 10 to 20, wherein the plunger includes a snap lock to secure the plunger within the housing after removal of the safety clip. 22. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 10 to 21, wherein the first disengagement means i ncludes plunger front end cooperative with movement of the plu nger at the front end of the housing for enabling disengagement of the engaged disposition between the needle hub with the housing ; said plunger front endslides with the plunger over the snap lock of the need le hub and forces the snap lock of the needle hu b to compress and disengage from the lock of the housing cap; said disengaged needle assembly automatically retracting within the housing along with the cannula with the assistance of the compressed spring expansion . 23. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 10 to 22, wherein the plunger portion inside said housing also retracts back with the needle assembly under its engagement with the needle assembly. 24. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as clai med in anyone of clai ms 10 to 23, wherein the second engagement means i ncludes a locking 

 means on the label holder to permanently couple the needle assembly in the housing rendering the needle assembly useless. 25. A safety housi ng based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of the claims 1 to 9, which is a twist based safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system comprising said housing comprising of an outer housing and an inner housing ; said needle assembly having the cannula at its front end with fluid communica ble connection with a su bsequent needle holder fully accommodated within the inner and the outer housing and supported with an expanded spring positioned between outer housing top and front end of the needle assembly; said plunger rod configured for the initial i njecting plunger forward motion to eject the needle/cannula through an opening i n the outer housing having " the releasable seal means to operatively couple the plunger rod with back end of the needle assembly and the inner housi ng ; and the first engagement means to engage the needle assembly with the outer housing ; and the subsequent continuing injecting plunger forwa rd motion to inject the i mplant through the ejected needle/cannula having the seal releasing means to disengage coupling between need le assembly, the inner housing and the plunger rod ; said first disengagement means for enabl ing disengagement of engaged disposition between the needle assembly and the outer housing ; said second engagement means on the needle holder to permanently arrest the need le assembly in the housing . 26. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in claim 25, wherein the outer housing is configured to fully enclose the needle assembly and the inner housing which coupled to back end of the outer housing is configured to telescopically move within the outer housing . said inner housing surrou nds the coupling point between the plunger rod and the back end of the need le assembly. 27. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in claim 25 or 26, wherein the plunger rod includes a plunger cap at its back end . 28. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 25 to 27, wherein the inner housing comprise reverse snap seated inside groove of the outer housing to arrest any rotation of the inner housing during ejection of the cannula from the outer housing . 29. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 25 to 28, wherein the releasable seal means involves a safety cap providing a butt joint between the plunger rod and the inner housing and the needle assembly which ensures that the plunger rod cooperatively move with respect to the inner housing and the needle assembly and any pushing force applied on the plu nger cap gets transferred to the inner housing and the needle assembly via the safety cap which drives the need le assembly surrounded with the i nner housing in forwa rd di rection th rough the outer housing with the plunger rod keeping the outer housi ng in static position ; said cooperative movement of the plunger and the needle assembly restrict plunger rod movement through the needle holder and thus prevent movement of the i mplant i ndependently with respect to the needle assembly during driving of the need le assembly. 30. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 25 to 29, wherein the inner housing includes a forward snap configu red to release by press for driving the inner housing into the outer housing to eject the cannula out of the outer housing . 31. A safety housing based implant/med icament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 25 to 30, wherein the first engagement means includes the reverse snap on the inner housing and forward snap lock in the outer housing; said reverse snap on the inner housing get snap locked with the forward snap lock in the outer housing when the cannula is completely ejected out along with the protective cap from the outer housing top with continued applying force on the plunger cap d riving the needle assembly with the inner housing within the outer housi ng towards the front end of the housing . 32. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 25 to 31, wherein the reverse snap lock on the inner housing get snap locked with the forward snap lock in the outer housing with an audible click sou nd compressing the spring to arrest reverse movement of the needle assembly with respect to the housing . 33. A safety housi ng based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 25 to 32, wherein forward movement of the needle assembly when the cannula is completely ejected out is arrested by having a butt joint between the i nner and the outer housi ng to facilitate piercing of the cannula after removal of the protective cap into the body/skin/tissue by holding the housing . 34. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as clai med in anyone of claims 25 to 33, wherein the seal releasing means i n back of the inner housing disengage the safety cap for removi ng and enabling the plunger rod moveably independent of the needle assembly so that further application of pushing 

 force on the plu nger cap drive said plunger rod in forward d i rection through the needle holder and push the implant through the cannu la into the tissue. 35. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as clai med in anyone of claims 25 to 34, wherein at the end of the injection the plunger cap irrecoverably sits within the inner housing pocket. 36. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 25 to 35, wherein the first disengagement means for enabling disengagement of engaged disposition between the needle assembly and the outer housing includes ci rcular snap between the needle assembly and the outer housing ; said circular snap enables integrated rotation of the inner housing, the plunger rod and the plunger cap with rotation of grip area on the inner housi ng and arrest rotation of the needle assembly; said rotation of the inner housing, the plunger rod and the plunger cap disengages the engagement between the reverse snap lock on the inner housing and the forward snap lock in the outer housing ; said disengaged needle assembly automatically retracting within the housing along with the cannula with the assistance of the compressed spring expansion. 37. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 25 to 36, wherein the second engagement means i ncludes lock between the needle holder and the forward snap lock which perma nently arrest the need le assembly in the outer housing rendering the needle assembly useless. 38. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of the claims 1 to 9, which is press type safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system comprising said housing preferably a cylindrical barrel with a housing top in its front end having small opening defined in the housing top; said needle assembly fully accommodated within the housing and supported with an expanded spring which is positioned between housing top and front end of the needle assembly having a needle hub comprising the cannula at its front end ; a label holder at its back end ; an implant container positioned in an axially symmetric manner between the label holder and the needle hub; said plunger means comprising a plunger irremovably secured inside the housing and the plunger rod insert molded within plunger body configured for the initial injecting plunger forward motion to eject the cannula through the opening having â¢ the releasable seal means to operativeiy couple the plunger and theplunger rod with the label holder of the needle assembly; and the first engagement means to engage the needle hub with the housing; and the subsequent continuing injecting plunger forward motion to inject the implant/medicament through the ejected cannula having the seal releasing means to disengage the coupling between the label holder and the plunger; said first disengagement means for enabling disengagement of the engaged disposition between the needle hub and the housing ; 

 said second engagement means to permanently arrest the needle assembly in the housing. 39. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in claim 38, wherein the needle assembly comprises a plunger rod guide running through the implant container in the axially symmetric manner between the label holder and the needle hub enabling the plunger rod to move through said plunger rod guide and push implant/medicament or the injectable substances which are prefilled in the implant container towards the needle hub. 40. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claim 38 or 39, wherein the cannula includes a fluid communicable connection with the plunger rod guide to receive the implant or the injectable substances, said cannula is adapted to eject through the small opening defined in the housing top and penetrate within the body or tissue to deliver the implant or the injectable substances. 41. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 38 to 40, wherein the label holder includes across flat surface at its back end configured to engage with inner surface of the housing to arrest any rotational movement of the needle assembly with respect to the housing and allow only spring biased forward and backward motion of the needle assembly within the housing. 42. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 38 to 41, wherein the releasable seal means includes a butt joint at back end of the label holder to couple plunger outer body with the needle assembly and ensure pushing force applied on the plunger gets transferred to the needle assembly via the butt joint and enabling cooperative forward movement of the 

 plunger and the needle assembly inside the housing keeping the housing in static position ; said cooperative movement of the plunger and the needle assembly restrict plunger rod movement through the plunger rod guide and thus prevent movement of the implant independently with respect to the needle assembly during driving of the needle assembly. 43. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 38 to 42, wherein the first engagement means comprises a snap lock on the needle hub configured to engage with cooperative locking portion in the housing top when the cannula is completely ejected out along with the protective cap from the housing with continued pushing force applied on the plunger causing the needle assembly to move towards the front end of the housing. 44. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 38 to 43, wherein the needle hub gets engaged with cooperative locking the housing top withan audible click compressing the spring arrest the forward and reverse movement of the needle assembly with respect to the housing and facilitate piercing of the cannula after removal of the protective cap into the body/skin/tissue by holding the housing . 45. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 38 to 44, wherein the label holder includes a snap lock which sits within the housing and freely to move in the forward direction, inside the housing to restrict the accidental reverse motion of the needle assembly. 46. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 38 to 45, wherein the seal releasing means comprises press button on the housing at a selected position between the front and back end of the housing ensuring front end of plunger outer body which is coupled with the label holder at the 

 butt joint reaches the reg ion of the press button in the housing when the needle hu b gets locked with cooperative locki ng portion i n the housing top. 47. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as clai med in anyone of claims 38 to 46, wherein the press button d isengages the butt joint between the label holder of the needle assembly and the plunger front end upon receiving a gentle force on it and enables the plunger moveably independent from the needle assembly to ensu re fu rther application of pushing force on the plunger d rive in forward direction in the housing independent of the need le assembly causing the plunger rod to move i n forward direction through the rod guide and push the implant through the cannula into the tissue . 48. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecti ng system as claimed in anyone of claims 38 to 47, wherein the first disengagement means includes the plunger front end cooperative with movement of the plunger towards front end of the housing for enabling disengagement of the engaged disposition between the needle hub and the housing ; said fi rst disengagement means includes sliding of the plunger front end over the snap lock of the needle hub which forces the snap lock of the needle hub to compress and disengage from the lock of the housing top at end point of the plunger movement; said disengaged need le assembly automatically retracting within the housing along with the cannula with the assistance of the compressed spri ng expansion . 49. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as clai med in anyone of claims 38 to 48, wherein the plunger portion inside the housing is also retracted back with the needle assembly as it has an engagement with the needle assembly. 50. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 38 to 49, wherein the second engagement means includes snap lock on the label holder to permanently arrest the needle assembly in the housing rendering the needle assembly useless. 51. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of the claims 1 to 9, which is hybrid type safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system comprising said housing comprising a top housing and a bottom housing axially coupled to each other said needle assembly having a front end accommodated within the top housing and a back end accommodated within the bottom housing and supported with an expanded spring which is positioned between top housing and the front end of the needle assembly, the needle assembly is comprising of a needle hub comprising the cannula at its front end; a label holder at its back end; an implant container positioned in an axially symmetric manner between the label holder said plunger means comprising a plunger irremovably secured inside the housing and the plunger rod insert molded within plunger body configured for the initial injecting plunger forward motion to eject the cannula through the opening having the releasable seal means to operatively couple the plunger and the plunger rod with the label holder of the needle assembly; and the first engagement means to engage the needle hub with the housing ; 

 and the subsequent continuing injecting plunger forward motion to inject the implant through the ejected cannula having the seal releasing means to disengage the coupling between the label holder and the plunger; said first disengagement means for enabling disengagement of the engaged disposition between the needle hub and the top housing ; said second engagement means to permanently arrest the needle assembly in the bottom housing . 52. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in claim 51, wherein the needle assembly comprises a plunger rod guide running through the implant container in the axially symmetric manner between the label holder and the needle hub enabling the plunger rod to move through said plunger rod guide and push implant/medicament or the injectable substances which are prefi I led in the implant container towards the needle hub. 53. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claim 51 or 52, wherein the cannula includes fluid communicable connection with the plunger rod guide to receive the implant/medicament or the injectable substances, said cannula is adapted to eject through the small opening defined in the housing top and penetrate within the body or tissue to deliver the implant or the injectable substances. 54. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 53, wherein the label holder includes across flat surface at its back end configured to engage with inner surface of the top and bottom housing to selectively arrest any rotational movement of the needle assembly with respect to the top and the bottom housing. 55. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 54, wherein the across flat surface engagement of the needle assembly with inner surface of the top housing and the inner surface of the bottom housing arrest rotational movement of the bottom housing with respect to the top housing prior ejection of the cannula from the top housing. 56. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 55, wherein the releasable seal means includes a butt joint at back end of the label holder to couple plunger outer body with the label holder of the needle assembly and ensure pushing force applied on the plunger gets transferred to the needle assembly via the butt joint and enabling cooperative forward movement of the plunger and the needle assembly inside the housing; said cooperative movement of the plunger and the needle assembly restrict plunger rod movement through the plunger rod guide and thus prevent movement of the implant independently with respect to the needle assembly during driving of the needle assembly. 57. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 56, wherein the label holder includes a snap configured sits within both the top and the bottom housing and freely to move in the forward direction, inside the housing to restrict the accidental reverse motion of the needle assembly. 58. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 57, wherein the first engagement means comprises a snap lock on the needle hub configured to engage with cooperative locking portion in front of the top housing when the cannula is completely ejected out along with the protective cap from the housing with continued pushing force applied on the plunger causing the needle assembly to move towards the front end of the housing . 59. A safety housing based > implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 58, wherein the needle assembly engaged with the top housing as the snap lock of the needle hub gets locked with cooperative locking portion in front of the top housing with an audible click compressing the spring, arrest the forward and reverse movement of the needle assembly with respect to the housing and facilitate piercing of the cannula after removal of the protective cap into the body/skin/tissue by holding the housing. 60. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 59, wherein the top and the bottom housings are selectively dimensioned to ensure the joining surface between the top and the bottom housing lies on the butt joint between the plunger and the needle assembly when the needle hub gets locked with cooperative locking portion in front of the top housing. 61. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 60, wherein the seal releasing means comprises across flat surface releasing gap in housing inner surface at the joint of the top and the bottom housings to enable rotation of the top housing and the needle assembly w.r.t the bottom housing and disengage the butt joint between the plunger and the label holder with an audible click sound; said disengagement of the butt joint enabling the plunger to move independent from the needle assembly to ensure further application of pushing force on the plunger drive in forward direction in the housing independent of the needle assembly causing the plunger rod to move in forward direction through the rod guide and push the implant through the cannula into the tissue. 62. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 61, wherein the bottom housing includes a lock feature to restrict counter rotation the top housing once it is rotated in a particular direction for disengaging the butt joint. 63. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 62, wherein the first disengagement means includes the plunger outer body cooperative with movement of the plunger at front end of the housing for enabling disengagement of the engaged disposition between the needle assembly and the top housing involving sliding of the plunger outer body over the snap lock of the needle hub and at end point of the plunger movement the plunger front end which cooperative with movement of the plunger forces the snap lock of the needle hub to compress and disengage from the lock of the housing top; said disengaged needle assembly automatically retracting within the housing along with the cannula with the assistance of the compressed spring expansion. 64. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 63, wherein the plunger portion inside the housing is also retracted back with the needle assembly as it has an engagement with the needle assembly. 65. A safety housing based implant/medicament injecting system as claimed in anyone of claims 51 to 64, wherein the second engagement means includes snap lock on the label holder to permanently arrest the needle assembly in the bottom housing rendering the needle assembly useless.
